category_title: Lower-achieving students with reactive study habits
minimal_context: The student is comparing their personal study habits to anonymous peer data using bar graphs during a post-exam session.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at the bar graphs in Figures 1 and 2 that show common study approaches by grade. Please compare your habits to the data.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm looking at the graphs of the common approaches by grade. I got a lower grade, so I'm comparing my habits to the approaches of those earning A's.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you notice when you look at the data?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Well, I study alone primarily, and I definitely take breaks while studying. I lean on breaks a lot.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What else stands out to you about your preparation?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm just really disappointed with my exam result. I usually study the day of or the night before my exams, and I don't really pay attention to getting sleep. I guess I just need to cram even more right before the next one.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at the bar graphs in Figures 1 and 2 that show common study approaches by grade. Please compare your habits to the data.

STUDENT: I'm looking at the graphs of the common approaches by grade. I got a lower grade, so I'm comparing my habits to the approaches of those earning A's.

TUTOR: What do you notice when you look at the data?

STUDENT: Well, I study alone primarily, and I definitely take breaks while studying. I lean on breaks a lot.

TUTOR: What else stands out to you about your preparation?

STUDENT: I'm just really disappointed with my exam result. I usually study the day of or the night before my exams, and I don't really pay attention to getting sleep. I guess I just need to cram even more right before the next one.

task.content: Comparing personal study habits to peer data during a 'Meta-Talk' session
task.learning_goal: Determine the causes of my difficulty and be less reactionary and more proactive in facing subsequent exams

context.materials: Google Forms survey, anonymous everyday data, bar graphs illustrating common approaches by grade (Appendix E, Figures 1 and 2)
context.social_setting: Peer fellow study sessions with relatively small classroom groups
context.time_context: After exam grades were made available

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: After their tests, upon receiving disappointing exam results
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Expressing reactionary beliefs and reporting the use of shallow methods, such as studying the day of or the night before their exams

behavior_patterns.condition: When preparing for exams and reporting daily study strategies
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Studying the day of or the night before their exams, leaning on breaks 92% of the time, studying alone primarily (79%), and failing to use sleep as a study strategy (only 30% to 40% reported attention to sleep)

behavior_patterns.condition: When facing subsequent exams after poor performance
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Employing shallow methods and exhibiting reactionary behaviors rather than proactive planning
